items.title: Google Slides: Gemini can now generate fully editable, brand-matched slide decks
items.why_it_matters: Turns prompt-to-deck into something teams can actually ship (editable, on-brand), reducing the last-mile manual formatting work.
items.brief: Gemini in Slides now generates editable, brand-matched decks with dynamic layouts.
items.details: Google rolled out an upgrade to Gemini in Google Slides that generates complete slide decks that remain fully editable. The feature emphasizes brand-matched styling and dynamic layouts tailored to your content, aiming to reduce the time spent on formatting and design cleanup. This is a practical step from ‘AI drafts’ to ‘AI deliverables’ for business presentations. Check rollout/eligibility details on the Workspace Updates post.

items.title: NVIDIA: GH200 sets STAC-ML record for single-digit microsecond latency inference
items.why_it_matters: Shows where GPU inference is heading for ultra-low-latency workloads (finance, real-time control), not just throughput benchmarks.
items.brief: NVIDIA details GH200 results hitting single-digit microsecond inference latency on STAC-ML.
items.details: NVIDIA published a deep dive on achieving single-digit microsecond latency inference for capital markets workloads, highlighting results on the STAC-ML benchmark using the GH200 Grace Hopper Superchip. The post focuses on end-to-end latency engineering rather than only tokens/sec, which is increasingly relevant for agentic systems that must act in real time. If you build latency-sensitive inference stacks, the tuning and system-level considerations here are the main value. The article includes the benchmark framing and the hardware/software setup at a high level.

items.title: Anthropic: Claude Code GitHub Action v1.0 now generally available
items.why_it_matters: Makes it easier to operationalize Claude Code in CI (PR automation, repo tasks) with a stable 1.0 release.
items.brief: Claude Code GitHub Action hits v1.0 GA for CI-based agent workflows.
items.details: Anthropic shipped a v1.0 release of the Claude Code GitHub Action, marking general availability. A GA milestone typically signals more stable interfaces and clearer operational expectations for teams wiring agentic coding into CI/CD. If you’re experimenting with PR review, refactors, or automated repo chores, Actions is the fastest path to productionizing those flows. Review the release notes for configuration and any breaking changes before upgrading.
